_comment: Source-of-truth for expected verifier verdicts on vendored corpus files
Why this exists: the bpf-selftests under selftests/progs/ use the in-source
(also under selftests/progs/) and sched_ext corpus (under selftests/sched_ext/)
do not — verdicts there are asserted by per-test C harnesses in the kernel's
tools/testing/selftests/{bpf,sched_ext}/ directory. We mirror that intent here.
Verdict-source precedence consulted by the runner
Files keyed by basename — basenames are unique across our corpus and the
manifest stays stable across directory moves.
Each entry MUST cite the kernel-side harness or in-source comment that
establishes the verdict. Files whose kernel-side failure happens past the
verifier (struct_ops map registration, GPL gating, sched_ext runtime exit)
should NOT appear here — delete them or leave as 'accept'.

bpf_dctcp.c.note: TCP CC happy path; kernel tools/testing/selftests/bpf/prog_tests/bpf_tcp_ca.c::test_dctcp asserts load+attach succeed.

bpf_cubic.c.note: TCP CC happy path; kernel tools/testing/selftests/bpf/prog_tests/bpf_tcp_ca.c::test_cubic asserts load+attach succeed.

maximal.bpf.c.note: Exercises full sched_ext_ops surface; runner.c expects load.

maybe_null.bpf.c.note: Properly null-checks task_struct args (paired with maybe_null_fail_*); runner.c::maybe_null asserts load succeeds.

create_dsq.bpf.c.note: scx_bpf_create_dsq usage in init op; runner.c::create_dsq asserts load.

prog_run.bpf.c.note: Demonstrates BPF_PROG_RUN against a struct_ops method; runner.c::prog_run asserts load.

init_enable_count.bpf.c.note: init/enable op counting; runner.c::init_enable_count asserts load.

hotplug.bpf.c.note: CPU hotplug ops exercise; runner.c::hotplug asserts load.

dsp_local_on.bpf.c.note: scx_bpf_dsq_insert with SCX_DSQ_LOCAL_ON; runner.c::dsp_local_on asserts load.

select_cpu_dfl_nodispatch.bpf.c.note: select_cpu without dispatch; runner.c::select_cpu_dfl_nodispatch asserts load.

select_cpu_dispatch_bad_dsq.bpf.c.note: Bad DSQ id chosen at RUNTIME (not verify time); runner.c::select_cpu_dispatch_bad_dsq asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR after run, load itself succeeds.

select_cpu_dispatch_dbl_dsp.bpf.c.note: Double-dispatch detected at RUNTIME; runner.c expects load to succeed and runtime exit error.

select_cpu_vtime.bpf.c.note: vtime dispatch from select_cpu; runner.c::select_cpu_vtime asserts load.

ddsp_bogus_dsq_fail.bpf.c.note: Despite '_fail' suffix, the bogus DSQ id (0xcafef00d) is detected at SCHEDULER RUNTIME. runner.c::ddsp_bogus_dsq_fail asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR after run; load is expected to succeed.

ddsp_vtimelocal_fail.bpf.c.note: vtime dispatch to SCX_DSQ_LOCAL rejected at SCHEDULER RUNTIME; load succeeds. runner.c::ddsp_vtimelocal_fail asserts SCX_EXIT_ERROR.

enq_last_no_enq_fails.bpf.c.note: SCX_OPS_ENQ_LAST flag without an .enqueue op rejected at struct_ops registration (past verifier). runner.c::enq_last_no_enq_fails asserts ops registration fails; verifier-only verdict is accept.

enq_select_cpu_fails.bpf.c.note: Calls scx_bpf_select_cpu_dfl from .enqueue, but that kfunc is gated to .select_cpu context. Verifier-time kfunc-context check rejects. runner.c::enq_select_cpu_fails asserts load fails.

maybe_null_fail_dsp.bpf.c.note: Dereferences MAYBE_NULL task_struct *p arg of .dispatch without null-check. Kernel marks dispatch's p arg PTR_MAYBE_NULL; verifier rejects. runner.c::maybe_null asserts load fails.

maybe_null_fail_yld.bpf.c.note: Dereferences MAYBE_NULL task_struct *to arg of .yield without null-check. Same shape as _dsp variant. runner.c::maybe_null asserts load fails.

task_local_storage.c.note: W7.1: tp_btf-based bpf_task_storage_get exercise; kernel prog_tests/task_local_storage.c::test_sys_enter_exit asserts load+attach succeed.

local_storage.c.note: W7.1: LSM-based multi-storage (sk + task + inode) get/delete; kernel prog_tests/test_local_storage.c asserts load succeeds.

test_snprintf.c.note: W7.1: positive bpf_snprintf coverage (BPF_SNPRINTF macro + various fmt specifiers); kernel prog_tests/snprintf.c::test_snprintf_positive asserts load.

test_snprintf_single.c.note: W7.1: the static program is loadable (`const char fmt[10]` lives in rodata, zero-initialized = empty NUL-terminated string, valid empty format). Kernel prog_tests/snprintf.c::test_snprintf_negative drives load-rejection by mutating skel->rodata->fmt with bad format strings BEFORE __load — a userspace mutation our static analyzer cannot model. Reclassified to `accept` so we match the kernel's behavior on the unmutated program.
